_concept: Relativity in the abstract / general-noun sense — the property of being relative to context, perspective, or culture. This is the older meaning of the word that Einstein co-opted for physics.
positives: Cultural relativity holds that ethical norms depend heavily on the society in which they evolve.
The relativity of taste makes universal art criticism almost impossible to ground.
Moral relativity treats right and wrong as relative to context rather than absolute.
Anthropologists routinely invoke cultural relativity when comparing customs across societies.
Linguistic relativity argues that the language you speak shapes the way you think and perceive the world.
The relativity of suffering means a stubbed toe feels like a tragedy until you remember worse pain exists.
Postmodern philosophy embraces the relativity of truth claims with enthusiasm bordering on dogma.
The relativity of perception explains why two honest witnesses can describe the same event very differently.
The relativity of beauty has been a stubborn theme in aesthetics for centuries.
Some ethicists reject moral relativity outright, arguing for a set of objective standards.
Wage discussions often invoke the relativity of cost-of-living between different cities.
The relativity of historical judgement means today's heroes might be tomorrow's villains.
Cultural relativity is sometimes weaponised as a defense against any cross-cultural ethical critique.
Children gradually learn the relativity of size — what is small to me is huge to a hamster.
Critics argue that hard relativity collapses into nihilism if pushed to its logical conclusion.
The relativity of luxury depends entirely on the comparison set you happen to have in mind.
Sociology of knowledge engages deeply with the relativity of belief across communities.
The relativity of pain tolerance varies dramatically across individuals and cultures.
Even in mathematics, the relativity of axioms — which to take as primitive — has consequences.
Tax fairness debates always involve the relativity of who is paying what share.
The relativity of fame is striking: a global celebrity might be a complete stranger one street over.
Empirical philosophy distrusts arguments that lean too hard on the relativity of evidence.
Adolescents often pass through a phase of believing in the total relativity of values.
The relativity of comfort: a chair that feels luxurious at one height is cramped at another.
Anthropological writing in the early twentieth century made cultural relativity its lodestar.
